topic: HL Regional Option: History of Africa & the Middle East
subject: history

items.q: Why did Stalin blockade West Berlin in 1948?
items.correct: To force the Western Allies out of Berlin in response to the merging of their zones, introduction of the Deutschmark, and creation of a separate West German state.
items.misconception: Stalin blockaded Berlin because he wanted to invade Western Europe.
items.random_wrong: Stalin blockaded Berlin to stop Nazis escaping to the West.
items.why: The blockade was reactive, not a step toward invasion. Students see all Soviet actions as offensive; in fact Berlin was a defensive move against Western consolidation.

items.q: What happened in the Russian Revolution of 1917?
items.correct: There were two revolutions: February overthrew the Tsar and installed the Provisional Government; October saw the Bolsheviks seize power from that government.
items.misconception: The Bolsheviks overthrew the Tsar in 1917.
items.random_wrong: Lenin personally led the storming of the Winter Palace.
items.why: Students collapse 1917 into one event. The Tsar abdicated in February under pressure from liberals and soldiers — the Bolsheviks took power eight months later from a republican government.

items.q: Why did the Provisional Government fail in 1917?
items.correct: It continued the unpopular war, did not redistribute land, shared 'dual power' with the Petrograd Soviet, and was discredited by the Kornilov affair before the Bolshevik takeover.
items.misconception: The Provisional Government fell because Lenin was personally more charismatic than Kerensky.
items.random_wrong: The Provisional Government fell because the Tsar returned and reclaimed his throne.
items.why: Its failure was structural, not personal: war, land and dual power. Students reduce political collapse to charismatic leadership when material grievances drove events.

items.q: Who led Ghana to independence in 1957?
items.why: It's Kwame Nkrumah. “Jomo Kenyatta” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Which 1962 accords ended the Algerian War?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Evian. “Geneva” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: How many African states gained independence in the Year of Africa?
items.why: Exact answer: 17 states. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
